pages.full_text:
We stopped at Ax to shop, a lovely big market, cheese, olives [Ve...]
cake, apricots, honey baskets. An Andean music group-
Simon bought 2 CDs, one for goddaughter, Rosie.
, Lots of thermal spas here. A king built one for
returning Crusaders who had leprosy.
	We drove on to Montaillou. It's a
small, attractive village, parts decayed, parts
repaired. The Cathars Bookshop was closed, Old [th...]
pages.notes: Upper portion of page contains a glued-in printed French leaflet/poster titled 'SUR QUELLES TRACES MARCHEZ-VOUS' (illustrated guide to animal tracks in the Pyrenees). Handwritten diary text continues below the leaflet. Right edge of handwritten text is cut off — first line ends with 'olives' then '[Ve...]' suggesting a word continues onto next line. Final line also cut: 'Old [th...]'. 'Ax' likely refers to Ax-les-Thermes, a thermal spa town in Ariège, France. No handwritten page number visible on this side.

pages.full_text:
Very well organised museum with workshops for children
springs on the hillside. Simon, Eve & Charles
walked up to the castle ruin (John went
off for a pee + failed to catch up with us)
There were 2 families having a picnic at
the foot of the castle. Donkey [saddles?] were
pages.notes: Upper portion of page continues the same glued-in French printed leaflet (same animal tracks poster, continuation from left page), with a large arrow and text 'RETROUVEZ LES PISTES DE CES ANIMAUX SUR LE CHEMIN'. Handwritten diary text continues below. The first line 'Very well organised museum with workshops for children' appears to be a continuation from left-page text (referencing Montaillou). The text at the bottom is cut off mid-sentence: 'Donkey [saddles?] were' — last word(s) unclear, possibly 'saddles' or 'saddled'. Page number 31 visible in top-right corner. People named: Simon, Eve, Charles, John.
